div.div: External caller

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 843px; margin-left: 246px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 10px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: nowrap;

div.div:
Bold name = repo exists today; plain = planned. Dashed-red frame = untrusted guest boundary (holds only a scoped JWT — no signing key, no backend key).
Bold orange = storage data path. ocu-filestore is the only door to storage: a file API to its consumers, a storage client to the pluggable engine (S3 off-box, or local volume on-box). Two consumers reach that API — the guest mount leg (the only leg through the egress MITM) and the host-side Web UI — never the engine directly.
Dashed lines = control / provisioning / trust-material / audit fan-in. There is no egress↔issuer edge: the egress passes the Bearer through untouched; the engine verifies the JWT and enforces scope.

a.text: Text is not SVG - cannot display
